mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations. A 25 basis point drop in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window triggers positive S&P 500 returns within the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines > 25 basis points over preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.59% on 2006-03-01 reflects mid-tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons per month signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns when PAYEMS growth momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134,966 thousand on 2006-03-01 shows strong employment base
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during positive PAYEMS growth periods do not exceed baseline by at least 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector demand. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, this interaction indicates weakening aggregate demand that compresses equity valuations within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,120 thousand and RSAFS at 361,046 million on 2006-02-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 2% year-over-year indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, supporting corporate profit margins. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 5-day forward returns when INDPRO momentum is positive.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.21 on 2006-03-01 reflects manufacturing activity
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns during positive INDPRO growth do not exceed negative-growth periods by at least 1% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary pressure that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase rates, compressing equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when inflation accelerates in a tightening cycle.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, equity valuations decline within 60 trading days due to earnings forecast revisions.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% on 2006-03-01 near cycle lows
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal aggregate demand strength. When both series show year-over-year growth above 3%, this interaction indicates robust economic expansion that supports equity valuations with positive 60-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 3% and RSAFS year-over-year growth > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,248.3 billion and RSAFS at 361,046 million on 2006-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in linear regression over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline below 1% year-over-year combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE > 5.0%) signals supply-side weakness and labor market stress. This interaction predicts negative 20-day forward returns when both conditions occur simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 111.21 and UNRATE at 4.7% on 2006-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of jointly-triggered instances, or interaction coefficient fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year rolling window.
